heading: Federal Permitting Improvement Steering Council
label: 4370m–1 Federal Permitting Improvement Steering Council
notes: CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Fixing America’s Surface Transportation Act, also known as the FAST Act, and not as part of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 which comprises this chapter.

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: a Establishment
children.text: There is established the Federal Permitting Improvement Steering Council.

children.label: A
children.text: be appointed by the President; and

children.label: B
children.text: serve as Chair of the Council.

children.heading: Designation by head of agency
children.label: i Designation by head of agency

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Each individual listed in subparagraph (B) shall designate a member of the agency in which the individual serves to serve on the Council.

children.heading: Redesignation
children.label: II Redesignation
children.text: If an individual listed in subparagraph (B) designates a different member to serve on the Council than the member designated under subclause (I), the individual shall notify the Executive Director of the designation by not later than 30 days after the date on which the designation is made.

children.heading: Qualifications
children.label: ii Qualifications
children.text: A councilmember described in clause (i) shall hold a position in the agency of deputy secretary (or the equivalent) or higher.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Consistent with guidance provided by the Director of the Office of Management and Budget, each individual listed in subparagraph (B) shall designate 1 or more appropriate members of the agency in which the individual serves to serve as an agency CERPO.

children.heading: Reporting
children.label: II Reporting
children.text: In carrying out the duties of the agency CERPO under this subchapter, an agency CERPO shall report directly to the applicable agency councilmember.

children.label: i
children.text: The Secretary of Agriculture.

children.label: ii
children.text: The Secretary of the Army.

children.label: iii
children.text: The Secretary of Commerce.

children.label: iv
children.text: The Secretary of the Interior.

children.label: v
children.text: The Secretary of Energy.

children.label: vi
children.text: The Secretary of Transportation.

children.label: vii
children.text: The Secretary of Defense.

children.label: viii
children.text: The Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency.

children.label: ix
children.text: The Chairman of the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission.

children.label: x
children.text: The Chairman of the Nuclear Regulatory Commission.

children.label: xi
children.text: The Secretary of Homeland Security.

children.label: xii
children.text: The Secretary of Housing and Urban Development.

children.label: xiii
children.text: The Chairman of the Advisory Council on Historic Preservation.

children.label: xiv
children.text: Any other head of a Federal agency that the Executive Director may invite to participate as a member of the Council.

children.heading: Additional members
children.label: 3 Additional members
children.text: In addition to the members listed in paragraphs (1) and (2), the Chairman of the Council on Environmental Quality and the Director of the Office of Management and Budget shall also be members of the Council.

children.label: i
children.text: not later than 180 days after , establish an inventory of covered projects that are pending the environmental review or authorization of the head of any Federal agency;December 4, 2015

children.label: I
children.text: categorize the projects in the inventory as appropriate, based on sector and project type; and

children.label: II
children.text: for each category, identify the types of environmental reviews and authorizations most commonly involved; and

children.label: iii
children.text: add a covered project to the inventory after receiving a notice described in .section 4370m–2(a)(1) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: designate a facilitating agency for each category of covered projects described in subparagraph (A)(ii); and

children.label: ii
children.text: publish the list of designated facilitating agencies for each category of projects in the inventory on the Dashboard in an easily accessible format.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Not later than 1 year after , the Executive Director, in consultation with the Council, shall develop recommended performance schedules, including intermediate and final completion dates, for environmental reviews and authorizations most commonly required for each category of covered projects described in subparagraph (A)(ii).December 4, 2015

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The performance schedules shall reflect employment of the most sound and efficient applicable processes, including the alignment of Federal reviews of projects, reduction of permitting and project delivery time, and consideration of the best practices for public participation.

children.heading: In general
children.label: aa In general
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, and consistent with applicable Federal law, the Executive Director, in consultation with the Council, shall aim to develop recommended performance schedules under clause (i) of not more than 2 years.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: bb Exception
children.text: If a recommended performance schedule developed under clause (i) exceeds 2 years, the relevant agencies, in consultation with the Executive Director and the Council, shall explain in that recommended performance schedule the factors that cause the environmental reviews and authorizations in that category of covered projects to take longer than 2 years.

children.heading: In general
children.label: aa In general
children.text: The final completion dates in any performance schedule for the completion of an environmental review or authorization under clause (i) shall not exceed the average time to complete an environmental review or authorization for a project within that category.

children.heading: Calculation of average time
children.label: bb Calculation of average time
children.text: The average time referred to in item (aa) shall be calculated based on relevant historical data, as determined by the Executive Director, and shall run from the period beginning on the date on which the Executive Director must make a specific entry for the project on the Dashboard under (except that, for projects initiated before that duty takes effect, the period beginning on the date of filing of a completed application), and ending on the date of the issuance of a record of decision or other final agency action on the review or authorization.section 4370m–2(b)(2) of this title

children.heading: Completion date
children.label: cc Completion date
children.text: Each performance schedule shall specify that any decision by an agency on an environmental review or authorization must be issued not later than 180 days after the date on which all information needed to complete the review or authorization (including any hearing that an agency holds on the matter) is in the possession of the agency.

children.heading: Review and revision
children.label: iii Review and revision
children.text: Not later than 2 years after the date on which the performance schedules are established under this subparagraph, and not less frequently than once every 2 years thereafter, the Executive Director, in consultation with the Council, shall review and revise the performance schedules.

children.label: i
children.text: to carry out responsibilities under this subchapter; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to effectuate the adoption by agencies of the best practices and recommendations of the Council described in paragraph (2).

children.heading: Personnel
children.label: E Personnel
children.text: The Executive Director of the Council may appoint and fix the compensation of such employees as the Executive Director considers necessary to carry out the roles and responsibilities of the Executive Director.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Council shall make recommendations to the Executive Director with respect to the designations under paragraph (1)(B) and the performance schedules under paragraph (1)(C).

children.heading: Update
children.label: ii Update
children.text: The Council may update the recommendations described in clause (i).

children.label: I
children.text: engaging with Native American stakeholders to ensure that project sponsors and agencies identify potential natural, archeological, and cultural resources and locations of historic and religious significance in the area of a covered project; and

children.label: II
children.text: fully considering and, as appropriate, incorporating recommendations provided in public comments on any proposed covered project;

children.label: ii
children.text: ensuring timely decisions regarding environmental reviews and authorizations, including through the development of performance metrics;

children.label: iii
children.text: improving coordination between Federal and non-Federal governmental entities, including through the development of common data standards and terminology across agencies;

children.label: iv
children.text: increasing transparency;

children.label: v
children.text: reducing information collection requirements and other administrative burdens on agencies, project sponsors, and other interested parties;

children.label: vi
children.text: developing and making available to applicants appropriate geographic information systems and other tools;

children.label: vii
children.text: creating and distributing training materials useful to Federal, State, tribal, and local permitting officials;

children.label: viii
children.text: in coordination with the Executive Director, improving preliminary engagement with project sponsors in developing coordinated project plans;

children.label: ix
children.text: using programmatic assessments, templates, and other tools based on the best available science and data; and

children.label: x
children.text: addressing other aspects of infrastructure permitting, as determined by the Council.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: C Meetings
children.text: The Council shall meet not less frequently than annually with groups or individuals representing State, tribal, and local governments that are engaged in the infrastructure permitting process.

children.label: A
children.text: advise the respective agency councilmember on matters related to environmental reviews and authorizations, including agency compliance with intermediate and final completion dates described in coordinated project plans;

children.label: B
children.text: provide technical support, when requested to facilitate efficient and timely processes for environmental reviews and authorizations for covered projects under the jurisdictional responsibility of the agency, including supporting timely identification and resolution of potential disputes within the agency or between the agency and other Federal agencies;

children.label: C
children.text: analyze agency environmental review and authorization processes, policies, and authorities and make recommendations to the respective agency councilmember for ways to standardize, simplify, and improve the efficiency of the processes, policies, and authorities, including by implementing guidance issued under paragraph (1)(D) and other best practices, including the use of information technology and geographic information system tools within the agency and across agencies, to the extent consistent with existing law; and

children.label: D
children.text: review and develop training programs for agency staff that support and conduct environmental reviews or authorizations.
